inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the notifier.

inputs.name: enabled
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether the notifier is enabled or not. Set to: true to enable the notifier. false to disable the notifier. This is the default. If a notifier is disabled, alerts are not fetched for the notifier.

inputs.name: consumptionMode
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether notifications are created for all alerts or only the latest alerts. Set to: earliest to get notifications for all alerts in the system. This is the default. latest to get notifications only for the alerts emitted after the notifier is created.

inputs.name: autoMark
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether notifications are automatically marked as read or a user must do it manually. Set to: true to automatically mark a notification as read after the service specified in the notifier settings is invoked. This is the default. false to manually dismiss a notification from Integration Server Administrator , which marks the notification as read.

inputs.name: channelIds
inputs.description: Identifies the channels for which you want to receive notifications. You can specify either an individual channel identifier or a list of identifiers. For example, channelIds[0] as 2 for "Server Error" or channelIds[1] as 10 for "Threat Protection" or channelIds[0] as 2 and channelIds[1] as 1 for both.

inputs.name: intervalInSeconds
inputs.description: Time in seconds after which the system task created for the notifier should check for unread alerts.

inputs.name: severityCutoff
inputs.description: The severity to consider for fetching alerts. For example, if you set this value as Critical , you will receive notifications only for critical events. If you set this value as Error , you will receive notifications for critical and error events. Set the parameter to one of the following values: Critical . This is the default. Error Information Warning

inputs.name: notifyInstantly
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether or not notifications are generated as soon as an alert is raised. Set to: true to instantly generate a notification for an alert. false if an instant notification is not required for an alert. This is the default. Note: If intervalInSeconds is also specified, the notifyInstantly parameter takes precedence.

inputs.name: webSocketEnabled
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether or not Integration Server uses a WebSocket connection to send notifications. Set to: true to enable a WebSocket connection to send notifications. false to disable a WebSocket connection. This is the default.

inputs.name: webSocketSender
inputs.description: Optional. Sends a message on a connected WebSocket session.

inputs.name: serviceInvokeEnabled
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether a service is invoked with the notifier parameters as pipeline variables or not. Set to: true to invoke a service using the notifier parameters. false to disable service invocation for the notifier. This is the default.

inputs.name: serviceInvokeSender
inputs.description: Optional. Invokes a service for alerts fetched for this notifier.

inputs.name: smtpEnabled
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether Integration Server uses an SMTP server to send notifications or not. Set to: true to use an SMTP server to send notifications. false if you do not want to use an SMTP server to send notifications. This is the default.

inputs.name: smtpSender
inputs.description: Optional. Details of the SMTP Server and client used to send the notifications.

outputs.name: message
outputs.description: Indicates whether the notifier is successfully created or not.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the notifier that you want to delete.

outputs.name: message
outputs.description: Indicates whether the specified notifier is deleted or not.

inputs.description: Name of the notifier that you want to disable.

outputs.description: Indicates whether the specified notifier is disabled or not.

inputs.description: Name of the notifier that you want to enable.

outputs.description: Indicates whether the specified notifier is enabled or not.

inputs.name: Channels
inputs.description: The list of alert channels in Integration Server . By default, there are 13 channels. All channels have the following keys:

outputs.name: Channels
outputs.description: The list of alert channels in Integration Server . By default, there are 13 channels. All channels have the following keys:

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Optional. To get the count of a subset of alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

outputs.name: count
outputs.description: The count of all alerts in the system, read and unread, or a subset of alerts, if the filter criteria are passed as inputs.

inputs.name: notifierName
inputs.description: Optional. Name of the notifier for which alerts are to be fetched. If you do not specify the notifier name, all unread alerts in the system or a subset of the unread alerts that meet the filter criteria are considered.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Optional. To get the count of a subset of unread alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

outputs.name: count
outputs.description: The count of all unread alerts in the system or a subset of unread alerts, if the notifier name and the filter criteria are specified.

inputs.name: channelId
inputs.description: Identifier of the channel that you want to deregister.

outputs.name: deregistered
outputs.description: Indicates whether the channel is deregistered or not. A value of: true indicates that the channel is deregistered. false indicates that the channel is not deregistered.

inputs.name: channelID
inputs.description: Identifier of the channel associated with the alert.

inputs.name: severity
inputs.description: Severity associated with the alert channel.

inputs.name: subject
inputs.description: Subject of the alert message.

inputs.name: contentType
inputs.description: Type of information in the alert. For example, text/plain .

inputs.name: content

outputs.name: alertId
outputs.description: A unique identifier assigned to each alert emitted.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Optional. To fetch a subset of alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

inputs.name: sortOrder
inputs.description: Optional. The order in which the fetched alerts are sorted. Select ASC or DESC . The default is DESC .

inputs.name: sortBy
inputs.description: Optional. The criteria to sort the fetched alerts. Select one of the following options: channel severity subject timestamp The default is timestamp .

inputs.name: pageSize
inputs.description: The number of notifications on a page.

inputs.name: pageNumber
inputs.description: In a paginated list of notifications, the page where Integration Server loads the notifications. Note: Page number starts with 0.

outputs.name: alerts
outputs.description: A list of the alerts retrieved by the service. Each alert has the following details:

outputs.name: id
outputs.description: Alert identifier.

outputs.name: ts
outputs.description: Date and time when the alert is generated, in the UNIX epoch time format.

outputs.name: serverId
outputs.description: IP address of the host where Integration Server is running.

outputs.name: memberName
outputs.description: Member name of the machine that hosts Integration Server in a cluster.

outputs.name: user
outputs.description: Integration Server user who triggered the alert.

outputs.name: channelId
outputs.description: Channel identifier for the alert.

outputs.name: channel
outputs.description: Channel for the alert.

outputs.name: severityId

outputs.name: severity
outputs.description: Severity of the alert.

outputs.name: subject
outputs.description: Subject of the alert message.

outputs.name: contentType
outputs.description: Text that identifies the type of content in the alert such as text/plain and text/html .

outputs.name: content
outputs.description: Content of the alert.

inputs.description: Optional. If you do not specify the notifier name, all the unread alerts in the system or a subset of the unread alerts that meet the filter criteria are fetched.

inputs.name: channelId
inputs.description: Identifier of the channel that you want to retrieve.

outputs.name: displayName
outputs.description: Name of the channel.

outputs.name: description
outputs.description: Description for the channel.

outputs.name: emissionEnabled
outputs.description: Indicates whether an alert is generated for the channel and stored in the database or not. A value of: true indicates that alerts are emitted for the channel and stored in the database. false indicates that alerts are disabled for the channel.

outputs.name: systemNotificationEnabled
outputs.description: Indicates whether an alert generated for the channel is displayed as a notification or not. true indicates that notifications are generated for the channel. false indicates that notifications are disabled for the channel.

inputs.name: enabled​Emission​Channels
inputs.description: List of channels for which alerts can be generated and stored in the database.

inputs.name: enabledSystem​NotifierChannels
inputs.description: List of channels for which alerts are generated and displayed as notifications.

inputs.name: system​Notifier​​Severity​Cutoff
inputs.description: Notifications are generated only for alerts with severity equal to, or more than this value.

inputs.name: retentionPeriod​InDays
inputs.description: Determines how long the alerts are stored in the database.

inputs.name: certificate​Expiry​​Warning​InDays
inputs.description: Number of days before the certificate expiry date, when the alert should be generated.

outputs.name: enabled​Emission​Channels
outputs.description: List of channels for which alerts can be generated and stored in the database.

outputs.name: enabledSystem​NotifierChannels
outputs.description: List of channels for which alerts are generated and displayed as notifications.

outputs.name: system​Notifier​​Severity​Cutoff
outputs.description: Notifications are generated only for alerts with severity equal to, or more than this value.

outputs.name: retentionPeriod​InDays
outputs.description: Determines how long the alerts are stored in the database.

outputs.name: certificate​Expiry​​Warning​InDays
outputs.description: Number of days before the certificate expiry date, when the alert should be generated.

inputs.name: notifierName
inputs.description: Optional. Name of the notifier for which all the alerts are to be marked as read. If you do not specify the notifier name, alerts of system channels are marked as read.

outputs.name: updateCount
outputs.description: Updated count of the unread alerts in the system.

inputs.name: alertId
inputs.description: Specifies the alert that should be marked as read.

inputs.name: notifierName
inputs.description: Optional. Name of the notifier for which for which the alert should be marked as read.

outputs.name: updateCount
outputs.description: Updated count of the unread alerts in the system.

inputs.name: alertId
inputs.description: The alert that you want to mark as unread.

inputs.name: notifierName
inputs.description: Optional. The notifier for which the alert should be marked as unread.

outputs.name: updateCount
outputs.description: Updated count of the unread alerts in the system.

inputs.name: displayName
inputs.description: Name of the channel that you want to register.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Optional. Description of the channel.

inputs.name: emissionEnabled
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether an alert is generated for the channel and stored in the database or not. Set to: true to emit alerts for the channel and store them in the database. false to disable alerts for the channel. The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

inputs.name: systemNotificationEnabled
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether the alert generated for the channel is displayed as a notification or not. Set to: true to generate notifications for the channel. false to disable notifications for the channel. The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

outputs.name: channelId
outputs.description: Identifier of the registered channel. Note: Channel IDs for custom channels start with 1001.

inputs.name: enabled​Emission​Channels
inputs.description: List of channels for which alerts can be generated and stored in the database.

inputs.name: enabledSystem​NotifierChannels
inputs.description: List of channels for which alerts are generated and displayed as notifications.

inputs.name: systemNotifier​​SeverityCutoff
inputs.description: Notifications are generated only for alerts with severity equal to, or more than this value.

inputs.name: retentionPeriod​​InDays
inputs.description: Determines how long the alerts are stored in the database.

inputs.name: certificate​Expiry​​WarningInDays
inputs.description: Number of days before the certificate expiry date when the alert should be generated.

outputs.name: message
outputs.description: Indicates whether the system notifier settings are updated or not.

inputs.name: Severities
inputs.description: The list of default severities available in the system. By default, 4 severities are configured.

outputs.name: Severities
outputs.description: The list of default severities available in the system. By default, 4 severities are configured.

inputs.name: channelId
inputs.description: Identifier of the alert channel that you want to update. Note: Channel IDs for custom channels start with 1001.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Optional. Description of the channel.

inputs.name: emissionEnabled
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether an alert is generated for the channel and stored in the database or not. Set to: true to emit alerts for the channel and store them in the database. false to disable alerts for the channel. The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

inputs.name: systemNotificationEnabled
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether the alert generated for the channel is displayed as a notification or not. Set to: true to generate notifications for the channel. false to disable notifications for the channel. The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

outputs.name: message
outputs.description: Indicates whether the channel is successfully updated or not.
